title: Shinjis 4th wall break narration in Episode 21
display_title: Shinjis 4th wall break narration in Episode 21
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's 4th wall break narration in Episode 21             Quote Post by Stuart » Wed Aug 18, 2021 2:03 am One of the weirdest bits of NGE for me has always been the weird narration that Shinji gives as the last lines of episode 21, "He was aware that he was still a child." He says (Netflix subs): "All I could do was hide from Misato. I knew there was nothing I could do. Nothing I could say. I was just a child." Although we hear Shinji's internal thoughts at various other points in the series, they're always his present thoughts. This, however, is in the past tense, and comes across as an adult Shinji looking back on this moment. Kind of like Kevin in the Wonder Years, to use a weird example. From what point in time is Shinji talking from? Why does this only happen once in the series? How old is Shinji when he is saying these words (i.e. no longer "just a child")? And more importantly, why the creators' choice to include this? Now that we have a canon adult Shinji for the first time, maybe finally we can have some explanation to this?

[]: Re: Shinji's 4th wall break narration in Episode 21             Quote Post by The Dragon Mask » Thu Aug 26, 2021 11:33 pm Like many little oddities like that, it's probably a consequence of the particular writer on that episode using that as a dramatic way to end the episode, without having a plan on how it'd pan out. A lot of the show was made up as it went along. The genius of NGE is how that crazy mix of off the cuff ideas somehow made a ton of sense at the end, which I contribute to Anno and the other writer/director's ability to keep track of almost all the loose ends. Except this one. And yeah it is weird. But it's a nice fan theory that this is the one instance of 28 year old Shinji narrating haha. Overall though I'd personally say it would be an adult Shinji in the original world (after EoE). Which hey, that implies he survives that long, yay!
